skill.name: code-reviewer
skill.description: Review local changes and remote pull requests with structured feedback on correctness, maintainability, tests, and security.
skill.summary: Perform structured code reviews for local changes and remote pull requests.
skill.author: google-gemini

security_audit.summary: The static shell-command findings are real command guidance, but they support legitimate code review workflows. The weak cryptography findings are false positives from prose in SKILL.md, and no prompt injection or exfiltration intent was found. Publication is reasonable with a warning because the workflow suggests checking out remote PRs and running project commands.

medium_findings.title: Remote PR Checkout and Project Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The workflow tells agents to use gh pr checkout for remote PRs and run npm run preflight. This is legitimate for code review, but it can execute scripts from untrusted repository changes if used without confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and semantically tied to reviewing remote PRs. The risk depends on repository trust and user approval, so confidence is high but not critical.

low_findings.title: Local Repository Command Guidance
low_findings.description: The workflow recommends git status, git diff, git diff --staged, and optional npm run preflight. These are normal review commands, but project scripts should run only with user approval.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible in review instructions and have legitimate intent. The remaining concern is limited to optional execution of project-defined scripts.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography matches occur in prose and examples, not in cryptographic code or dependency usage. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or insecure crypto operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found only natural-language instructions at the cited lines. There is no code implementing or invoking weak cryptography.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Several external command matches point to markdown fences or inline branch names, not executable instructions. No evidence found of hidden shell execution at those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain markdown syntax or branch-name examples after manual inspection. They do not instruct direct shell execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented External Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents shell commands for GitHub CLI, git, and npm. These are expected for code review, but they require normal safeguards for untrusted PR code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly present in markdown code blocks and inline command text. They are not hidden, obfuscated, or framed as persistence or exfiltration behavior.

content.user_title: Review Code Changes with Structured Findings
content.value_statement: Code reviews often miss edge cases, tests, and maintainability risks. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through a repeatable review process.
actual_capabilities: Reviews local staged and unstaged changes.
Reviews remote pull requests by number or URL.
Checks correctness, readability, maintainability, performance, and security.
Suggests missing tests and edge cases.
Structures feedback by severity and recommendation.
limitations: It does not prove code is free of defects.
Remote PR workflows may require GitHub CLI access.
Project preflight commands can run repository scripts.
Review quality depends on available project context.

use_cases.title: Pre-Merge Review
use_cases.description: Review a pull request before merging and identify blocking defects.

use_cases.title: Local Change Check
use_cases.description: Inspect staged or unstaged changes before opening a pull request.

use_cases.title: Quality Coaching
use_cases.description: Learn how a change can improve tests, readability, and maintainability.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Local Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my local changes. Focus on correctness, tests, and clear findings.

prompt_templates.title: Review Staged Changes Only
prompt_templates.prompt: Review only my staged changes. Separate blocking issues from suggestions.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Pull Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Review PR #123. Read the description, inspect the diff, and report findings by severity.

prompt_templates.title: Deep Review with Risk Focus
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a deep review of this change. Focus on security, edge cases, performance, and missing tests.

output_examples.input: Review my staged changes before I commit.
output_examples.output: The review highlights one blocking correctness issue, two test gaps, and one maintainability suggestion.

output_examples.input: Review PR #42 for security and regression risk.
output_examples.output: The review summarizes the PR goal, lists security concerns first, and recommends changes before approval.

output_examples.input: Check whether this refactor is safe.
output_examples.output: The review compares behavior before and after the refactor, then calls out untested edge cases.

best_practices: Ask before running project commands from untrusted pull requests.
Provide the PR number, URL, or exact local scope.
Include test results and project context when available.
anti_patterns: Do not treat automated review as final approval.
Do not run repository scripts from unknown code without consent.
Do not ask for broad review when only one file changed.

faq.question: Can this review remote pull requests?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports pull request numbers and URLs when the needed repository tools are available.

faq.question: Can it review local changes?
faq.answer: Yes. It can inspect staged changes, unstaged changes, or both.

faq.question: Does it run tests automatically?
faq.answer: It can suggest project preflight checks. Users should approve command execution first.

faq.question: What issues does it prioritize?
faq.answer: It prioritizes correctness, security, regressions, missing tests, maintainability, and performance.

faq.question: Is it safe for untrusted PRs?
faq.answer: Use caution. Reviewing diffs is safer than running scripts from untrusted code.

faq.question: Does it replace human review?
faq.answer: No. It helps find issues and organize feedback, but maintainers remain responsible for approval.
